ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT WNA TECHNOLOGIES, INC'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WNA TECHNOLOGIES, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO THE SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ADON NETWORK, LLC SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES BOTH OF ITS FRAUDULENT TRANSFER CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST WNA. "WHILE A COMPLAINT MUST CONTAIN MORE THAN A BARE ALLEGATION THE DEFENDANTS CONSPIRED, A COMPLAINT IS SUFFICIENT IF IT APPRISES THE DEFENDANT OF THE 'CHARACTER AND TYPE OF FACTS AND CIRCUMSTANCES UPON WHICH SHE WAS RELYING TO ESTABLISH THE CONSPIRACY.'" (AREI II CASES (2013) 216 CAL.APP.4TH 1004, 1022.) ADON ALLEGES FACTS AND CIRCUMSTANCES THAT PERMIT A REASONABLE INFERENCE THAT WNA WAS PART OF THE PLAN TO DEFRAUD ADON. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT MR. BRODEUR ORCHESTRATED THE FRAUDULENT TRANSFERS AND FORMED WNA DURING THE COURSE OF EVENTS THAT CONSUMMATED THE FRAUDULENT TRANSFERS. WNA DOES NOT NEED TO HOLD THE TRANSFERRED ASSETS TO BE LIABLE AS A CO-CONSPIRATOR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ADAPTIVE MEDIAS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ADAPTIVE MEDIAS, INC. IS DENIED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT DELAWARE LAW APPLIES TO THE ISSUE OF WHETHER THERE HAS BEEN A DE FACTO MERGER OF ONESCREEN INTO ADAPTIVE AND DELAWARE LAW REQUIRES THAT ADAPTIVE TO HAVE PURCHASED ALL OR SUBSTANTIALLY ALL OF ONESCREEN'S ASSETS AND ADAPTIVE TO HAVE ASSUMED ALL OR SUBSTANTIALLY ALL OF ONESCREEN'S LIABILITIES FOR THERE TO BE A DE FACTO MERGER, ADAPTIVE HAS NOT MEET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT IT DID NOT PURCHASE ALL OR SUBSTANTIALLY ALL OF ONESCREEN'S ASSETS OR THAT THAT IS DID NOT ASSUME ALL OR SUBSTANTIALLY ALL OF ONESCREEN'S LIABILITIES. THE ONLY FACTS THAT ADAPTIVE PROFFERS ON THESE POINTS ARE ITS FACTS 8 AND 9, BUT THOSE FACTS ARE LIMITED ONLY TO THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT, BUT DO NOT RULE OUT THAT ASSETS WERE PURCHASED OR LIABILITIES ASSUMED BY SOME MEANS OTHER THAN THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT. FURTHER, THE SUPPORTING EVIDENCE FOR FACTS 8 AND 9 ARE CONCLUSIONS, NOT EVIDENTIARY FACTS, AND ARE BASED IN LARGE PART ON THE "UNDERSTANDING" OF TWO DECLARANTS WHO DO NOT STATE THE BASES FOR THEIR UNDERSTANDING. EVEN IF ADAPTIVE HAD SATISFIED ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN, THERE ARE TRIABLE DISPUTES WHETHER ADAPTIVE PURCHASED ALL OR SUBSTANTIALLY ALL OF ONESCREEN'S ASSETS AND WHETHER ADAPTIVE ASSUMED ALL OR SUBSTANTIALLY ALL OF ONESCREEN'S LIABILITIES. (PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL FACTS 19-31, 36-41 AND 58). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ADON NETWORK, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES AND CLAIMS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE OPERATIVE PLEADING FOR THIS MOTION, DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT PLAINTIFF IS THE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ADON NETWORK, INC., MUCH LESS HOW IT CAME TO BE THE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE OPERATIVE PLEADING FRAME A SUMMARY MOTION AND SUCH A MOTION CANNOT BE GRANTED BASED ON PROOF OF ALLEGATIONS NOT PLED. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE IN ITS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT IT IS THE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ADON NETWORK, INC. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WNA TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR TRANSFER OF VENUE TO ORANGE COUNTY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT WNA TECHNOLOGIES, INC. CLAIMS THAT IT HAS A STATUTORY RIGHT TO HAVE THIS CASE HEARD IN ORANGE COUNTY. IN OTHER WORDS, THE ARGUMENT IS THAT AS A MATTER OF LAW THIS COURT IS AN IMPROPER VENUE. THIS COURT IS A PROPER VENUE UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 395.5. PLAINTIFF ADON NETWORK, LLC AND DEFENDANT ONESCREEN, INC. ENTERED INTO A CONTRACT (THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT) IN SAN FRANCISCO, THE CONTRACT WAS TO BE PERFORMED IN SAN FRANCISCO AND THE ALLEGED BREACH (I.E., PAYMENTS NOT RECEIVED BY ADON) OCCURRED IN SAN FRANCISCO. THE SEVEN CLAIMS STATED IN THE SAC INVOLVE PURSUIT OF A SINGLE PRIMARY RIGHT, I.E., ADON'S RIGHT TO PAYMENT UNDER THE CONTRACT, AND THE MAIN RELIEF SOUGHT IS PAYMENT UNDER THE CONTRACT. THE NON-ONESCREEN PARTIES ALLEGED LIABILITY IS THAT THEY ARE SUCCESSORS OR IN EFFECT SUCCESSORS TO ONESCREEN, THE THEORY BEING THAT THESE PARTIES INVOLVED THEMSELVES IN THE CONTRACT WHEN ADAPTIVE ACQUIRED ONESCREEN'S ASSETS DURING THE CONTRACT PERIOD. UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES, THE COUNTY WHERE THE CONTRACT WAS MADE, WHERE IT IS TO BE PERFORMED, WHERE LIABILITY ARISES AND WHERE THE BREACH OCCURRED IS AN APPROPRIATE VENUE. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC., 395.5.) GIVEN THAT THIS CASE INVOLVES A SINGLE PRIMARY RIGHT, IT IS NOT A MIXED ACTION. WNA DOES NOT HAVE A STATUTORY RIGHT TO HAVE THIS CASE HEARD IN ORANGE COUNTY. THE COURT REJECTS ADON'S ELECTION THEORY; THIS MOTION TURNS ON AN ANALYSIS OF THE COMPLAINT AND THE RELEVANT STATUTES. THUS, ITS MOTION FAILS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 =302/JQ

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Katherine O'Neal, of Futterman Dupree Dodd Croley Maier LLP, Tel. 415.399.3840, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Sasha N. Brower, of Macias Counsel Inc., Tel. 626.844.7707, for defendants WNA Technologies, Inc., appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WNA TECHNOLOGIES, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the sixth and seventh causes of action is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. Plaintiff Adon Network, LLC sufficiently alleges both of its fraudulent transfer causes of action against WNA. "While a complaint must contain more than a bare allegation the defendants conspired, a complaint is sufficient if it apprises the defendant of the 'character and type of facts and circumstances upon which she was relying to establish the conspiracy.'" (Arei II Cases (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 1004, 1022.) AdOn alleges facts and circumstances that permit a reasonable inference that WNA was part of the plan to defraud AdOn. The second amended complaint alleges that Mr. Brodeur orchestrated the fraudulent transfers and formed WNA during the course of events that consummated the fraudulent transfers. WNA does not need to hold the transferred assets to be liable as a co-conspirator. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Transfer Of Venue To Orange County; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL A. CROLEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-399-3845 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SASHA N. BROWER OF THE MACIAS COUNSEL INC., TEL. NO. 1-626-844-7707 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant WNA Technologies, Inc.'s motion for transfer of venue to Orange County is DENIED. Defendant WNA Technologies, Inc. claims that it has a statutory right to have this case heard in Orange County. In other words, the argument is that as a matter of law this court is an improper venue. This court is a proper venue under Code of Civil Procedure section 395.5. Plaintiff AdOn Network, LLC and Defendant OneScreen, Inc. entered into a contract (the settlement agreement) in San Francisco, the contract was to be performed in San Francisco and the alleged breach (i.e., payments not received by AdOn) occurred in San Francisco. The seven claims stated in the SAC involve pursuit of a single primary right, i.e., AdOn's right to payment under the contract, and the main relief sought is payment under the contract. The non-OneScreen parties alleged liability is that they are successors or in effect successors to OneScreen, the theory being that these parties involved themselves in the contract when Adaptive acquired OneScreen's assets during the contract period. Under these circumstances, the county where the contract was made, where it is to be performed, where liability arises and where the breach occurred is an appropriate venue. (See Code Civ. Proc., 395.5.) Given that this case involves a single primary right, it is not a mixed action. WNA does not have a statutory right to have this case heard in Orange County. The court rejects AdOn's election theory; this motion turns on an analysis of the complaint and the relevant statutes. Thus, its motion fails. Order signed in open court. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/jq

PLAINTIFF ADON NETWORK, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues And Claims is DENIED in its entirety. The first amended complaint, the operative pleading for this motion, does not allege that plaintiff is the successor in interest to AdOn Network, Inc., much less how it came to be the successor in interest. The allegations of the operative pleading frame a summary motion and such a motion cannot be granted based on proof of allegations not pled. Plaintiff is granted leave to allege in its second amended complaint that it is the successor in interest to AdOn Network, Inc. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Adaptive Medias, Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Peter T. Haven of Haven Law (213-842-4617) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/moving party, Adaptive Medias, Inc.

Attorney, Sean E. Macias of Macias Counsel Inc. (626-844-7707) appears as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party, Onescreen, Inc., et al.

No other appearance is made in this matter

Court hears no objection. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ADAPTIVE MEDIAS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Adaptive Medias, Inc. is DENIED. Assuming without deciding that Delaware law applies to the issue of whether there has been a de facto merger of OneScreen into Adaptive and Delaware law requires that Adaptive to have purchased all or substantially all of OneScreen's assets and Adaptive to have assumed all or substantially all of OneScreen's liabilities for there to be a de facto merger, Adaptive has not meet its burden of showing that it did not purchase all or substantially all of OneScreen's assets or that that is did not assume all or substantially all of OneScreen's liabilities. The only facts that Adaptive proffers on these points are its Facts 8 and 9, but those facts are limited only to the Stock Purchase Agreement, but do not rule out that assets were purchased or liabilities assumed by some means other than the Stock Purchase Agreement. Further, the supporting evidence for Facts 8 and 9 are conclusions, not evidentiary facts, and are based in large part on the "understanding" of two declarants who do not state the bases for their understanding. Even if Adaptive had satisfied its summary judgment burden, there are triable disputes whether Adaptive purchased all or substantially all of OneScreen's assets and whether Adaptive assumed all or substantially all of OneScreen's liabilities. (Plaintiff's Additional Facts 19-31, 36-41 and 58). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.
Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, Bus. Tel: 415-717-9768. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT ADAPTIVE MEDIAS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. The court reads the Complaint as a whole. The pleadings assert that as a result of the stock purchase agreement, Defendant acquired more than Media Graph stock. Specifically, Plaintiff alleges that as a result of the stock purchase agreement, Plaintiff obtained the right to purchase 100% of the outstanding shares of OneScreen, the shareholders of OneScreen were paid with shares of Adaptive, and Defendant purchased OneScreen's furniture and fixtures, office and computer equipment, software licenses, patents and trademarks, contracts with customers and suppliers, and intellectual property. (Ex. B Stock Purchase Agreement, Schedule 2.01). Thus, the pleadings and the documents filed with it, clearly describe facts which support an allegation of a de facto merger, resulting in Defendant assuming successor liability for OneScreen, Inc. Defendant's demurrer to the first, second, third, and fourth causes of actions is also denied. Plaintiff is not alleging that Defendant's actions have provided it with the bases to raise those causes of action. Rather, as Plaintiff clearly states in 9 of the complaint, Plaintiff seeks to hold Defendant, as OneScreen Inc.'s successor in liability, jointly and severally liable for any damages resulting to it from the alleged wrongful actions of OneScreen Inc. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

Motion For Discovery Sanctions (The Only Remaining Issue Re: Plaintiff Adon Network Llc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Sanctions Against Defendant Onescreen, Inc.)
Off Calendar Aug-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SEAN E. MACIAS OF THE MACIAS COUNSEL INC., TEL. NO. 1-626-844-7707 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Motion For Discovery Sanctions (The Only Remaining Issue Re: Plaintiff Adon Network Llc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Sanctions Against Defendant Onescreen, Inc.) - Request for sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.040's requirement of "setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought." It was not sufficient to merely request a dollar amount of sanctions, without explaining how that dollar amount was derived. Furthermore, the Court finds that Defendant acted with substantial justification in opposing the motion, because the opposition was based on its request for a protective order, a request which the Court granted. (See Foothill Properties v. Lyon/Copley Corona Associates (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1542, 1558.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
